_doc.max_concurrent: Max parallel agent sessions. Slot accounting is by LIVE tmux sessions, not Asana state. Env override: AGENT_WATCHER_MAX_CONCURRENT.
_doc.metro_base_port: Slot N gets Metro port (metro_base_port + N). 8181 (NOT 8081) keeps slot Metro ports out of the hardcoded DEBUG_<dep> dev-server range (8080/8082/8083/8084/8101) so a slot's Metro never collides with a DEBUG_<dep> webpack dev-server. Env override: AGENT_METRO_BASE_PORT.
_doc.master_sim: The iOS 18 iPhone 16 Pro Max sim holding the test account; cloned per slot. Resolved by device+runtime unless a UDID is hardcoded. refresh_on_develop (default true): before each spawn tick, refresh-master-build.sh checks if develop_ref advanced and, if its native side (ios/Podfile.lock) changed, rebuilds the master from develop and reclones the pool so runs test against a current build (BLOCKING; a JS-only develop advance skips the rebuild). develop_ref (default origin/develop): the branch the master tracks. Set refresh_on_develop=false or env SKIP_MASTER_REFRESH=1 to disable.
_doc.resource_guardrail: Pre-spawn safety gate. Skip spawning this tick if 1-min load avg > max_load_avg OR free RAM < min_free_ram_gb. Env overrides: AGENT_WATCHER_MAX_LOAD_AVG, AGENT_WATCHER_MIN_FREE_RAM_GB.
_doc.npm_migration_commit: Commit cherry-picked onto each fresh agent worktree so RN tooling uses npm not yarn.
_doc.default_repo: Repo a spawned agent works in (worktree + sim are provisioned for it).
_doc.sim_pool: Pre-cloned iOS sims kept ready so per-task spawn is fast (no inline simctl clone). Size defaults to max_concurrent. Lifecycle: free → in_use (on allocate) → dirty (on watchdog reap) → free (next ensure-sim-pool refresh). Refresh deletes the stale sim and clones from master.
_doc.keep_completed_worktrees: How many completed/retired worktrees to keep on disk for inspection/resume before the watchdog prunes the oldest. On completion the sim + slot are freed immediately; the worktree is retained. Worktrees whose session is still alive (incl. a RETIRED session) are never counted or pruned. Default 5; set 0 to destroy on completion (old behavior).
_doc.keep_completed_sessions: How many COMPLETED tmux sessions to keep alive for inspection / remote re-engagement before the watchdog kills the oldest. On completion a session is RETIRED — renamed claude-asana-<gid> → done-asana-<gid> so it no longer holds a concurrency slot — but its claude is left running so you can attach to it. Each retired session holds a live claude, so this bounds memory. Default 3; set 0 to hard-kill on completion (old behavior).
_doc.agent_model: Model forced onto spawned agent sessions via `claude --model <value>`. Empty/absent = the server-side default alias (resolved per turn; can change when Anthropic promotes a new default). Set to an exact ID to pin fleet behavior — e.g. claude-opus-4-8[1m] for Opus 4.8 with 1M context. A human can still flip a LIVE session's model per-turn via the desktop/RC picker; this only sets what sessions START on.
